name: Blueberry Summer Pudding
recipeIngredient: 8 pieces white bread slices, crusts removed
1 1/2 teaspoons cornstarch dissolved in liquid (rum, Grand Marnier, or orange juice)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, stir together blueberries, sugars, and orange juice. Simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
2. In a 1-1/2 quart bowl sprayed with butter-flavored nonstick spray, mold bread to the bottom and sides of the bowl. Spoon the cooled blueberry mixture over the bread. Layer more bread and spoon sauce over until all bread and sauce have been used or the bowl is filled.
3. Cover the bowl with a double layer of sprayed plastic wrap. Place a plate on top of the bowl and put a weight, such as a full can of food, on top. Refrigerate overnight.
4. To unmold the pudding, run a small, sharp knife around the inside of the bowl and invert it onto a serving plate.
5. Orange Sauce: In a saucepan, bring orange juice and sugar to a boil over medium heat. Whisk in cornstarch slurry and continue cooking until it thickens slightly. Cool the sauce, pour it into the bowl, cover, and refrigerate until ready to serve. The sauce can be served at room temperature or chilled.
296 calories, 6% fat, 2.1g fat
